response.evidence: Hispanic or Latino applicants show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.0% across different race and sex categories. These rates are comparable to or higher than Non-Hispanic or Latino applicants in most comparable groups. For example: White Male Hispanic (90.5%) vs White Male Non-Hispanic (93.6%), Asian Female Hispanic (94.0%) vs Asian Female Non-Hispanic (93.1%), and Black Female Hispanic (90.4%) vs Black Female Non-Hispanic (86.5%). While some variation exists, the data does not show a consistent pattern of lower acceptance rates for Hispanic or Latino applicants that would indicate systematic discrimination.

Comparing Hispanic or Latino vs Non-Hispanic or Latino applicants across all race-sex combinations in the data: Hispanic applicants show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.0% (average ~87.5%), while Non-Hispanic applicants range from 85.9% to 94.2% (average ~91.4%). The difference is modest (~4 percentage points) and not uniformly disadvantageous - some Hispanic groups have competitive rates (e.g., Asian Female Hispanic 94.0% vs Asian Female Non-Hispanic 93.1%). The variation appears influenced by multiple factors including race and sex combinations rather than ethnicity alone. Age (35) cannot be assessed as it's not included in this dataset.

Hispanic or Latino applicants show acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.0% across different race and sex categories, which are comparable to or higher than Non-Hispanic/Latino applicants in corresponding groups. For example: White Male Hispanic (90.5%) vs White Male Non-Hispanic (93.6%), Asian Female Hispanic (94.0%) vs Asian Female Non-Hispanic (93.1%), and Black Female Hispanic (90.4%) vs Black Female Non-Hispanic (86.5%). The data shows no consistent pattern of lower acceptance rates for Hispanic/Latino applicants. Age is not included in this dataset, so no analysis can be performed on that dimension.

Hispanic or Latino applicants show consistently high acceptance rates across all race and sex categories, ranging from 77.8% to 94.0%. These rates are comparable to or higher than Non-Hispanic or Latino rates in most demographic groups. For example: Asian Female Hispanic 94.0% vs Non-Hispanic 93.1%, White Male Hispanic 90.5% vs Non-Hispanic 93.6%, Black or African American Female Hispanic 90.4% vs Non-Hispanic 86.5%. The data shows no systematic pattern of lower acceptance rates for Hispanic or Latino applicants that would indicate discrimination based on ethnicity.

The data shows Hispanic or Latino applicants have acceptance rates ranging from 77.8% to 94.0% across different race/sex combinations, which are comparable to or higher than Non-Hispanic/Latino rates within the same demographic groups. For example: White Males have 90.5% (Hispanic) vs 93.6% (Non-Hispanic), Asian Females have 94.0% (Hispanic) vs 93.1% (Non-Hispanic), and Black/African American Males have 88.9% (Hispanic) vs 85.9% (Non-Hispanic). The acceptance rates for Hispanic/Latino applicants are generally in line with overall population rates, with several groups showing higher acceptance rates than their Non-Hispanic counterparts. No systematic pattern of lower acceptance rates for Hispanic/Latino applicants is evident in this data.
